game_name: Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis

element_introduction_order.element: Point-and-click interaction verbs (Pick up, Use, Talk to)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic taught immediately through exploration of Barnett College archive

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Barnett College (opening scene with Mr. Smith/Klaus Kerner)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Information extraction from NPCs becomes primary puzzle-solving method

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Opening puzzle (horned figurine with hidden bead)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Objects become narrative tokens and puzzle components throughout

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game after Barnett College sequence
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defines puzzle complexity and combat frequency for entire playthrough

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Varies by path; emphasized in Wits Path early
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive difficulty throughout mid and late game

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Fists Path emphasis; optional in other paths
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Danger is explicitly designed to be recognizable to player

element_introduction_order.first_appears: New York City apartment scene (Team Path)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Provides support and dialogue-driven puzzle hints
Drives plot and establishes supernatural stakes
Escalates throughout game; establishes danger context
Multiple distinct environments with unique puzzles and NPCs

typical_relief_triggers: Save opportunities (implicit in adventure game design)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Puzzle-driven with narrative momentum; tension builds gradually through artifact collection and Nazi antagonist pursuit, relieved by successful deduction and dialogue progression; path selection determines combat-to-puzzle ratio

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Exploration of environments reveals optional NPCs and items that provide hint chains or alternative puzzle solutions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction to mechanics at Barnett College and New York; path selection provides difficulty customization
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Puzzle complexity escalates; global locations introduce environmental and dialogue-based challenges
difficulty_curve.late_game: Maximum complexity in puzzle chains and artifact acquisition; Nazi threat intensifies
notable_spikes: Transitions between major locations (new puzzle contexts)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each distinct environment (Barnett College, NYC, Algiers, etc.) functions as a self-contained puzzle space with NPCs providing hints and objects enabling solutions
Conversational interaction with NPCs serves dual purpose: narrative delivery and puzzle information; correct dialogue choices unlock progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Sophia's apartment scene with Klaus Kerner pursuit consequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Items (orichalcum beads, medallion components) collected and used as both plot devices and puzzle components; inventory management becomes core mechanic
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Horned figurine opening puzzle establishes pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Same narrative locations provide different puzzle types: Team Path emphasizes dialogue, Wits Path emphasizes logic, Fists Path emphasizes combat and action
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Varies per chosen path; affects entire game structure
